description_long: This object is a sleek, modern airplane with a sharply angular fuselage and a low-profile design. The wings are positioned low on the body and extend straight out with a slight forward sweep, featuring a pair of prominent, flat surfaces. The tail section is distinctive, with twin vertical stabilizers set at the ends of a horizontal tailplane, forming an H-tail configuration. The nose is pointed and streamlined, with a metallic gray section suggesting a reinforced or specialized tip. The overall surface is smooth and dark, with a glossy finish that reflects light subtly. No visible engines or landing gear are present, giving the airplane a futuristic and minimalistic appearance.
description: A sleek, angular airplane with low-mounted, swept wings and a twin vertical stabilizer H-tail, finished in glossy black with a metallic nose.
description_view_0: Viewed from the front, the airplane appears narrow with a pointed nose and the twin vertical stabilizers barely visible.
description_view_1: From the left side, the low-mounted, swept wings and the H-tail configuration are clearly visible, along with the metallic nose.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the symmetry of the fuselage and the twin vertical stabilizers at the tail's ends.
description_view_3: From the right side, the design mirrors the left, highlighting the angular wings and the distinctive tail structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, slightly forward-swept wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers at the ends of a horizontal tailplane.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit integrated into the fuselage with a single canopy.
